faqs.q: Is PPF better than ELSS for tax saving?
faqs.a: Both PPF and ELSS qualify for Section 80C deduction up to ₹1.5 lakh. PPF is better if you want guaranteed, completely tax-free returns with zero risk. ELSS is better if you want potentially higher returns and can tolerate equity market volatility. Your choice depends on your risk appetite and investment horizon.

faqs.q: Can I invest in both PPF and ELSS?
faqs.a: Yes, you can invest in both PPF and ELSS. However, the combined Section 80C deduction limit is ₹1,50,000 per year. Many financial advisors recommend splitting your 80C allocation — for example, ₹75,000 in PPF for safety and ₹75,000 in ELSS for growth potential.

faqs.q: What are the returns of PPF vs ELSS in 2026?
faqs.a: PPF currently offers a guaranteed 7.1% p.a., compounded annually, which is fully tax-free. ELSS returns depend on the stock market — historical 5-year average returns for top ELSS funds range from 12-18% CAGR, but these are not guaranteed and can be negative in bad years.

faqs.q: Which has a shorter lock-in — PPF or ELSS?
faqs.a: ELSS has a much shorter lock-in of just 3 years, making it the most liquid 80C investment option. PPF has a 15-year lock-in, though partial withdrawals are allowed from the 7th year. If liquidity is your priority, ELSS is clearly better.

faqs.q: Is ELSS risky compared to PPF?
faqs.a: Yes, ELSS invests in equity markets and carries significant risk — your investment can lose value in the short term. PPF is backed by the Government of India with guaranteed returns and zero risk. However, over a 10-15 year horizon, ELSS has historically delivered significantly higher returns than PPF.

faqs.q: What happens to PPF and ELSS at maturity?
faqs.a: PPF matures after 15 years, and the entire maturity amount (principal + interest) is fully tax-free. ELSS units can be redeemed after 3 years. Long-term capital gains above ₹1.25 lakh on ELSS are taxed at 12.5%. Below ₹1.25 lakh, ELSS gains are also tax-free.

faqs.q: What is Section 80C and how much can I save?
faqs.a: Section 80C of the Income Tax Act allows individuals to claim deductions up to ₹1,50,000 per financial year from their taxable income. This means if you are in the 30% tax bracket, you can save up to ₹46,800 in taxes (including cess). Investments like PPF, ELSS, NSC, NPS, tax-saving FD, and life insurance premium qualify under 80C.

faqs.q: Which is the best tax saving investment for 2026?
faqs.a: The best 80C investment depends on your goals: PPF for guaranteed tax-free returns with zero risk, ELSS for highest growth potential with 3-year lock-in, and NPS for retirement planning with extra ₹50K deduction. For most people, a combination of PPF + ELSS provides the best balance of safety and growth.

faqs.q: Can I invest in multiple 80C options?
faqs.a: Yes, you can invest in multiple 80C instruments. The total deduction across all 80C investments is capped at ₹1,50,000 per year. Many investors split their allocation — for example, ₹75K in PPF + ₹75K in ELSS, or ₹50K each in PPF, ELSS, and NPS.

faqs.q: Which 80C investment gives the highest returns?
faqs.a: ELSS mutual funds have historically delivered the highest returns among 80C investments at 12-18% CAGR over 5 years. However, these returns are market-linked and not guaranteed. For guaranteed returns, PPF offers 7.1% (fully tax-free) and NSC offers 7.7% (taxable interest).

faqs.q: What is the safest tax saving investment?
faqs.a: PPF, NSC, SSY, and EPF are the safest 80C investments as they are all government-backed with guaranteed returns. Among these, PPF offers the best combination of safety + tax efficiency due to its EEE (Exempt-Exempt-Exempt) status, meaning deposits, interest, and maturity are all tax-free.

faqs.q: Is NPS better than PPF for tax saving?
faqs.a: NPS offers an additional ₹50,000 deduction under Section 80CCD(1B) over and above the ₹1.5L limit of 80C, potentially saving ₹15,600 extra in taxes (30% bracket). However, NPS is locked till age 60 and partially taxable. PPF is more flexible with EEE status. For maximum tax saving, invest in both.

SSY is an excellent option if you have a girl child under 10 years of age. It offers 8.2% p.a. (higher than PPF) with full EEE tax-free status. The 21-year lock-in is long, but partial withdrawal is allowed after the girl turns 18 for education expenses.

faqs.q: Should I invest in ULIP for tax saving?
faqs.a: ULIPs are generally not recommended solely for tax saving. They have high charges (mortality, fund management, administration) that eat into returns. ELSS offers similar equity exposure with lower costs and shorter lock-in. ULIPs may suit those who specifically want insurance + investment in one product.
